name: CEO / Owner-Founder
description: Owners and founders of NL-based scale-ups with revenue of €1M–€20M, growing fast but with a finance function that is still informal — built reactively as the company grew.
painPoints: Finance grew organically — no structure, no rhythm, no forecast
Owner makes capital and cash decisions on gut feel
Need a CFO but can't yet justify the permanent hire
decisionCriteria: Senior finance experience — not a junior accountant or generic adviser
Hands-on operator who builds the function, not just recommends
Plain language — financial findings explained without jargon
Structured process: diagnosis first, then roadmap, then execution
Credible handover plan so they are not dependent forever
skepticism: Has heard vague CFO-advisory pitches before. Wants to see a clear process, a concrete deliverable at each step, and a founder who has actually run finance inside a real business under pressure.
adaptedMessage: Wij brengen rust, overzicht en financiële grip — met een diagnose die benoemt wat er echt misgaat, een roadmap die laat zien hoe je dat oplost, en een CFO die het ook daadwerkelijk uitvoert.

topObjection.objection: We hebben al een boekhouder / accountant — waarom hebben we nog een externe CFO nodig?
topObjection.response: Een boekhouder verwerkt de cijfers; een accountant controleert ze. Wat ontbreekt is iemand die de cijfers gebruikt om beslissingen te nemen: wanneer heb je extra financiering nodig, welke klant is onrendabel, hoe ziet je cashflow er over zes maanden uit? Dat is de CFO-laag die de meeste scale-ups missen.

name: Board Chair / Lead Investor
description: Board chairs and lead investors at PE-backed portfolio companies or investor-backed scale-ups requiring interim CFO leadership during transitions — fundraise, M&A, leadership vacancy.
painPoints: Portfolio company in transition — fundraise, M&A, or CFO vacancy — with no senior finance cover
Board reporting is insufficient for investment-grade decisions
Finance team lacks a senior leader who can represent the company to investors and banks
Need a credible CFO face during due diligence or a transaction process
decisionCriteria: Senior finance track record that holds up in due diligence
Experience representing a company to investors and banks
Speed of mobilization for time-sensitive transitions
Clear handover plan to a permanent hire
skepticism: Investors want a senior CFO who has been in the room with banks and private equity before — not a generalist consultant. Needs to see WPP/H&K-level professional-services CFO experience.
adaptedMessage: A senior CFO with nearly 30 years of experience inside professional-services and media businesses — available as interim cover during your transition, with a structured handover to your permanent hire.

topObjection.objection: We need a permanent CFO, not an interim.
topObjection.response: A permanent CFO hire takes 3–6 months. During that window, the finance function cannot stand still — especially if you are in a fundraise or M&A process. Wilgert covers the seat, keeps the finance function running at CFO standard, and hands over to the permanent hire with a documented operating system.
